response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong geospatial expertise and distributed systems experience valuable for routing data pipelines, the lack of modern C++ or Rust production experience is a critical gap for a mid-senior IC role requiring immediate contribution. The 11-year gap in systems-level programming, combined with no routing/navigation domain background, presents significant ramp-up risk for a role shipping critical infrastructure at scale.
strengths: Deep geospatial data expertise: founded GISCollective (geo-data platform), presented at FOSS4G, built production GIS applications at Green Map
Proven distributed systems experience: Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/second with real-time data, low-latency design, and high-performance databases
Strong testing and quality culture: created fluent-asserts testing library and trial test runner; consistent TDD emphasis across roles
concerns: No recent C++ or Rust production experience: last C++ work was 11 years ago at National Instruments; primary requirement explicitly states modern C++ is critical
No routing or navigation domain expertise: geospatial work focuses on environmental/conservation mapping, not navigation guidance or routing optimization
Current tech stack divergence: most recent 2+ years are TypeScript/Node.js backend and full-stack; significant gap from systems-level programming
